name: J. Doe
bio: I study emergent structure in complex systems — how simple rules make complicated things. By night I build small interactive experiments like this desk. Currently a postdoc at the Institute for Placeholder Studies.

hero.title: Hello, I'm J. Doe
hero.body: Physicist, tinkerer, and occasional builder of odd little worlds like this one. Have a look around the desk — the papers, the mug, the old laptop. Everything's clickable.

book.title: Bardo Thodol
book.author: The Tibetan Book of the Dead

papers.title: Emergent Structure in Deep Latent Spaces
papers.abstract: We show that latent representations in deep generative models self-organise into geometric structures that mirror the symmetries of their training data, and give a practical probe for measuring this alignment.

papers.title: On the Thermodynamics of Small Systems
papers.authors: J. Doe, B. Colleague, C. Third
papers.abstract: Fluctuation theorems are extended to driven mesoscopic systems with measurement feedback, with an experimental protocol realisable in optical tweezer setups.

papers.title: A Bayesian Treatment of Observer Bias in Citizen Science
papers.abstract: A hierarchical model that separates observer skill from detection probability in large volunteer-collected datasets, validated on ten years of bird survey records.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Coupled Oscillator Networks
papers.abstract: Synchronisation thresholds in heterogeneous oscillator networks follow a universal scaling collapse across five orders of magnitude in network size.

projects.name: VECTRIS
projects.blurb: 3D neon Tetris with a global leaderboard — three.js, WebGL, way too much bloom.

projects.name: Desktopper
projects.blurb: This desk. A customisable 3D portfolio scene — fork it, swap portfolio.json, done.

welcome.title: Welcome
welcome.body: Hi — I'm J. Doe. Make yourself at home. Poke around the desk, or open the laptop for my papers, talks and side projects.

laptop.about: I'm a physicist working on emergent structure in complex systems. This machine holds my writing, presentations and projects — the tidy version of the clutter on the desk.
